strategic_profile.executive_summary: UCLA is a premier public land-grant research university located in the Westwood neighborhood of Los Angeles. As one of the most applied-to universities in the nation, it combines elite academic rigor with a vibrant campus life, Division I athletics, and extensive undergraduate research opportunities.
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic review utilizing 13 specific UC factors, with a strong emphasis on academic rigor and context rather than standardized testing.

analyst_takeaways.insight: UCLA receives over 145,000 applications annually, driving its acceptance rate below 9%.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must demonstrate near-perfect unweighted GPAs (median 4.0) and maximum course rigor to be competitive.

analyst_takeaways.insight: UCLA is completely test-blind for admissions.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Extracurricular achievements, Personal Insight Questions (PIQs), and GPA carry the entirety of the application's weight.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The College of Letters and Science does not admit by major at the freshman level.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applying to an 'obscure' major within L&S does not yield a strategic advantage; however, applying to L&S over Engineering or Arts significantly improves overall admission odds.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant campus culture with over 1,000 student organizations, robust Greek life, and a highly spirited athletic environment competing in the Big Ten Conference.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Located in Westwood with close proximity to Santa Monica and downtown LA, offering vast off-campus professional and recreational opportunities, though local housing costs and traffic are notable challenges.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 institution with very high research activity, heavily integrating undergraduates into medical, engineering, and social science research initiatives.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-blind/Test-free. The UC system does not consider ACT or SAT scores for admissions decisions or scholarship awards, though they may be used for post-enrollment course placement.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist admit rate calculated based on the number of students who accepted a spot on the waitlist.

gpa.notes: Median unweighted GPA for admitted students is 4.0. 56% of enrolled students had a 4.0 unweighted GPA.

testing.policy_note: UCLA is entirely test-blind. Standardized test scores are not used in admissions.

colleges.name: College of Letters and Science
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students interested in humanities, social sciences, or life/physical sciences. Major choice is not a factor in freshman admission, making it a safer entry point than the professional schools.
colleges.housing_profile: Standard undergraduate housing on the Hill.
colleges.student_archetype: Intellectually curious students seeking a broad liberal arts foundation combined with top-tier research.

majors.name: Business Economics
majors.special_requirements: Requires specific courses offered by the department and the John E. Anderson Graduate School of Management.
majors.notes: Highly sought-after major serving as an alternative to a traditional undergraduate business degree.

colleges.name: Samueli School of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Highly quantitative students with maximum rigor in high school math and physics. Extremely competitive.
colleges.housing_profile: Standard undergraduate housing on the Hill.
colleges.student_archetype: Technically driven innovators and aspiring engineers.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Internal transfers must maintain a 3.5 GPA in core math/science prep courses to even be considered.
majors.notes: One of the most competitive majors at UCLA. Transferring into CS from another school is highly restricted and subject to capacity.

colleges.name: School of Theater, Film and Television
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Creative students with demonstrated talent and a strong portfolio. Extremely selective.
colleges.housing_profile: Standard undergraduate housing on the Hill.
colleges.student_archetype: Aspiring filmmakers, actors, and industry professionals.

majors.name: Film and Television
majors.special_requirements: Requires a supplemental application including a personal essay (2-page max), life challenge essay, and creative portfolio.
notable_professors: Dr. Peter Shushtari (Acting and Performance in Film)
majors.notes: The most impacted program at UCLA.

application_deadlines.notes: The UC system does not offer Early Action or Early Decision.

supplemental_requirements.details: Applicants must respond to 4 out of 8 Personal Insight Questions (PIQs), each with a 350-word maximum.

supplemental_requirements.target_program: School of Theater, Film and Television
supplemental_requirements.details: Requires an extensive creative portfolio, personal essay, and life challenge essay.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: UCLA employs a 13-factor comprehensive review. Contextualizing academic achievement relative to the applicant's high school opportunities is crucial.

major_selection_tactics: For the College of Letters & Science, major choice is completely irrelevant to the admissions decision. Do not attempt to game the system by picking an obscure L&S major.
If applying to highly impacted programs like Engineering or TFT, know that you are evaluated exclusively against other applicants to those programs.
If undecided but leaning toward STEM, applying to L&S for Math or Physical Sciences provides an easier entry point than applying directly to the Samueli School of Engineering.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: UCLA normally does NOT consider alternate majors for admission into the College of Letters & Science if you are rejected from your first choice. For Engineering, an alternate major may be considered but only if it is also within the School of Engineering.

financials.tuition_model: Flat annual rate; differential for in-state vs out-of-state

scholarships.name: UCLA Regents Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: $2,000 annual honorarium regardless of need, priority enrollment (up to 10 units), guaranteed for 4 years.

ap_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for scores of 3, 4, or 5.
exceptions: In the College of Letters & Science, AP credit cannot be used to satisfy General Education requirements.
ap_policy.usage: Can be used to earn elective units, waive lower-level prerequisites, and improve enrollment standing (pass times).

ib_policy.general_rule: Accepted for credit depending on the specific department and exam level.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Transfer applicants must use ASSIST.org for articulation. UCLA caps lower-division transfer credits at 105 quarter units (70 semester units).

what_it_takes: Near-perfect unweighted GPA with maximum course rigor.
Compelling and highly personal UC PIQ essays that align with the 13 factors of comprehensive review.
Demonstrated impact or leadership in extracurricular activities.
essay_tips: Use the essays to expand on activities, not repeat them.
Show passion, depth, and authentic personal insight.
Use the 'Additional Comments' section strictly to explain contextual challenges or gaps in the academic record.
red_flags: Applying to highly impacted majors (like CS) without top-tier math/science grades.
insights: Getting off the waitlist is notoriously unpredictable and fluctuates wildly year-by-year (ranging from 2% to 13%).
Switching into Computer Science post-enrollment is incredibly difficult and heavily restricted.
